evidence.physics_rationale: Rear pushrod move changes static rake support and rear platform stability.
Front heave rate is driven by travel margin, clean bottoming, and aero-platform control.
Front heave perch sets the static slider position and available heave travel.
Rear heave support is a tradeoff between rear platform control and exit traction compliance.
Front wheel rate changes are justified by low-speed balance and body-slip stability.
Rear wheel support balances traction retention against low-speed rotation support.
Front camber is tied to contact patch shape, carcass temperature, and understeer support.
Rear camber balances traction stability against tyre support and heat distribution.
Brake bias is adjusted from braking lock evidence and braking-platform stability.
Diff preload is set from exit traction demand and rotation stability.
TC gain follows measured rear power-slip demand.
TC slip target tracks the measured rear power-slip envelope.
Brake bias target is passed through from hardware/session context because the solver only solves static brake bias.
Brake migration is carried forward as hardware context; no migration model is currently solved by the pipeline.
Front master cylinder sizing is treated as pass-through hardware context rather than a solved setup output.
Rear master cylinder sizing is treated as pass-through hardware context rather than a solved setup output.
Pad compound is reported honestly as pass-through brake hardware context, not as a solved telemetry output.

constraints.name: RARB sensitivity within useful range
constraints.note: <0.005 = insensitive (wrong bar size), >0.05 = too sensitive (step down)

car_specific_notes: Stiffer RARB -> more load transfer to rear -> front gains grip via LLTD. Use RARB as the primary live balance variable.
Cold tyre out-lap: softer RARB to prevent snap oversteer.

constraints.name: Front dynamic camber at peak g
Rear dynamic camber at peak g
Rear toe non-destabilizing
constraints.note: Rear toe-out is destabilizing — keep at or above 0mm

constraints.name: Front conditioning to op temp
constraints.note: Vision tread: >20 laps indicates tyres may not reach temp in a stint

notes: Representative cornering: 3.06g (p95=2.07g, peak=3.49g, kerb_weight=0.7). Roll: 2.1° at representative, 2.3° at peak.
Tyre temperature spread diagnosis: inner hotter = correct camber. If outer runs hotter -> reduce negative camber by 0.2-0.3 deg.
Porsche 963: target tyre temp 85-105°C (Michelin GTP compound). Conditioning typically 10-15 laps from cold start.
For sprint qualifying: add 0.2-0.3° more negative camber + 0.2mm extra toe-out to accelerate thermal buildup.

step6_dampers.hs_slope_reasoning: BASELINE — zeta uncalibrated. Returning validated baseline clicks: front LS=7/HS=5, rear LS=6/HS=3. Run click-sweep IBT session to unlock physics-derived values.
notes: BASELINE ONLY — zeta_is_calibrated=False for this car.
front LS=7 HS=5 | rear LS=6 HS=3
Perform dedicated click-sweep session to calibrate zeta targets.

supporting.brake_bias_reasoning: Calibrated base: 46.0% | Fuel correction: -0.03% at 44L | Result: 46.0%; +0.8% from trail brake depth p95=1.00; +0.2% for high braking decel p95=2.01g; -0.5% for front braking lock proxy p95=0.071; ⚠ High brake deceleration asymmetry (35.3 m/s²) detected — check front caliper/pad condition before adjusting brake bias; -0.2% for front brake wheel decel asymmetry p95=35.3 m/s^2; Note: measured hydraulic split 80.0% vs recommended 46.3% (delta +33.7%); MC: F/R = 20.6/22.2 mm (ratio 0.93); differs from nominal 1.00 — effective bias may need decrease; Pad: Medium — low brake temps, softer compound may improve initial bite | front-lock evidence shifted brake hardware and migration rearward
supporting.brake_hardware_status: Static brake bias is solved from telemetry; brake target, master cylinders, pad compound, and migration were conservatively seeded from braking evidence.
supporting.diff_reasoning: Base: 23.4 Nm (lat transfer=11706N at peak lat_g=3.49g); +5 Nm for rear power slip p95=0.088; clutch plates=6 | Coast 45 deg (from trail brake depth=0.37); Drive 70 deg (from throttle R^2=0.69) | Lock: coast=61.2% drive=26.2% (preload 6.1% + plates 55.1%)
supporting.tc_reasoning: Baseline: gain=4, slip=3; +1 gain for erratic consistency; +1 slip for rear power slip p95=0.088; +1 gain: TC barely active (0%) but rear slip p95=0.088; +1 slip for high MGU-K torque 321 Nm
supporting.pressure_reasoning: Track temp 19°C → cold adj +3.2 kPa; LF/RF hot split: 177/172 kPa → cold 153/155; Rear hot 170 kPa → cold 155 kPa
